mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in payroll employment above 100k month-over-month indicates strong labor market demand, driving consumer spending and positive equity returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS growth > 100k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 800k signals weak demand for construction, reducing corporate earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with HOUST < 800k are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > 0.05 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged increase in CPI above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, raising real rates and reducing equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in CPI > 2.5% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < -0.03 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production below 95% of trend indicates weak supply chain capacity, reducing corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in INDPRO < 95% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDP growth and rising unemployment signals economic contraction, creating a negative feedback loop that reduces equity valuations over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in GDP/UNRATE contraction periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in initial jobless claims below 200k signals labor market resilience, supporting consumer confidence and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in UNRATE < 200k periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged increase in the federal funds rate above 4% combined with rising inflation expectations creates a 'tightening spiral,' reducing equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in FEDFUNDS > 4% and CPI > 2.5% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < -0.03 over 3-year windows.
